---
title: "Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay"
chapter_id: "artifact-graphs-audit-logs-and-replay"
part_id: "planning-memory-reasoning-execution"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "talos"
  - "viea"
  - "cognitive_compilation"
  - "spinoza_composer"
  - "genesiscode"
  - "cognitive_loop_closure"
  - "moecot"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `artifact-graphs-audit-logs-and-replay` |
| Part | Part II - Planning, Memory, Reasoning, and Execution |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `talos`, `viea`, `cognitive_compilation`, `spinoza_composer`, `genesiscode`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The stack needs durable artifacts and replayable traces so work can be inspected, reused, tested, and improved.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

If outputs are not tied to inputs, tools, context, claims, and logs, later verification and improvement cannot know what happened.

## Core claim

[artifact-graphs-audit-logs-and-replay.core, support: argument] Execution should produce an artifact graph with audit logs, provenance, replay metadata, and links to claims and tests.

## Mechanism

- Record artifacts as nodes with source, job, context, tool, and evidence links.
- Capture replay metadata and environment assumptions.
- Use artifacts as memory and improvement substrate.

## Interfaces

- VCM references artifacts.
- Evidence consumes logs.
- Procedural memory mines repeated traces.

## Invariants

- Artifacts have stable identities.
- Audit logs are append-only or versioned.
- Replay limits are declared.

## Failure modes

- Untraceable output.
- Non-replayable workflow.
- Provenance gaps.

## Minimal implementation

An artifact graph schema with job, source, context, and evidence edges.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Artifact traceability test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Audit reconstruction test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Replay metadata completeness test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `cognitive_compilation` | Cognitive Compilation | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Compiler framing for LLM-centered planning, semantic IR, target compilation, incremental repair. |
| `spinoza_composer` | Spinoza Composer / Spinoza Trinity | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Narrative/compliance operating system variant. Use as applied Spinoza. |
| `genesiscode` | GenesisCode | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Tiny pure calculus + obligations + provenance for auditable AI-symbiotic programming. |
| `cognitive_loop_closure` | Cognitive Loop Closure | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Repeated cognition should become procedural memory / verified tools. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
